# GoTG - llms.txt

> Structured overview of the GoTG Telegram framework for AI agents and LLMs.

## Project Metadata
- **Name**: GoTG (Go Telegram)
- **Module**: github.com/pageton/gotg
- **Version**: v1.0.0-beta25
- **Type**: Go library (MTProto Telegram framework)
- **License**: GPL v3.0
- **Go Version**: 1.25.0+
- **Upstream**: Fork of celestix/gotgproto (v1.0.0-beta22)

## Description
High-level Telegram bot and userbot framework built on gotd/td. Provides intuitive APIs for session management, update handling, media operations, conversations, i18n, and Telegram Business API support.

## Project Structure
```
gotg/
├── adapter/          # Update handling, context, message operations, conversations
├── dispatcher/       # Handler dispatching, filters, middleware
├── session/          # Session management (SQLite, in-memory, string sessions)
├── storage/          # Peer storage, crypto, backends (memory, GORM, Redis)
├── parsemode/        # MarkdownV2/HTML parsing and formatting
├── log/              # Structured logging (zap-based, per-update loggers)
├── i18n/             # Internationalization (Fluent/YAML, 142 languages)
├── conv/             # Conversation state machine
├── ext/              # Extensions (GORM, Redis storage adapters)
├── functions/         # Utility functions (dump, peer resolution)
├── examples/         # 13+ example bots and use cases
└── root package      # Client constructor, core types, session encryption
```

## Key Packages
### Root Package (`gotg`)
- `NewClient(apiID, apiHash, clientType, opts)` - Primary client constructor
- `ClientOpts` - Configuration for sessions, logging, middlewares, reconnection
- `AsBot(token)` / `AsUser(phone)` - Client type constructors
- `Client` - Main client with Dispatcher, ConvManager, Self user info

### `adapter` Package
- `Update` - Wraps all Telegram update types with helper methods
- `Context` - Request context with Raw client, Sender, Entities, Logger
- `CommandRegistry` - Fluent API for registering bot commands
- `Update` methods: `Reply()`, `SendMessage()`, `StartConv()`, `Dump()`

### `dispatcher` Package
- `NativeDispatcher` - Default update dispatcher
- `handlers` - 19 handler types (OnMessage, OnCommand, OnBusiness*, etc.)
- `filters` - Message, chat, entity, and business filters

### `session` Package
- `SqlSession()`, `InMemorySession()`, `StringSession()` - Session constructors
- `NewSessionStorage()` - Creates peer + session storage from config

### `storage` Package
- `PeerStorage` - Caches user/chat/channel access hashes
- `Backend` interface - Pluggable storage (memory, GORM, Redis)

## API Highlights
### Quick Start (Bot)
```go
client, err := gotg.NewClient(apiID, apiHash, gotg.AsBot(token), &gotg.ClientOpts{
    InMemory: true,
})
dp := client.Dispatcher
dp.AddHandler(handlers.OnCommand("start", func(u *adapter.Update) error {
    u.Reply("Hello!")
    return nil
}))
client.Idle()
```

### Key Features
1. **Session Management**: Import from Pyrogram/Telethon/Gramjs, SQLite/in-memory/string sessions
2. **19 Update Handlers**: Messages, edits, deletions, reactions, boosts, business API
3. **Conversation System**: Multi-step state machine with timeouts and filters
4. **Structured Logging**: Per-update loggers, dual console+file output
5. **i18n**: 142 languages, Fluent/YAML formats, CLDR plural rules
6. **Outgoing Tracking**: Synthetic updates for sent/edited/deleted messages
7. **Performance**: Optimized string operations, bounded goroutines, DB write fan-in

## Links
- Repository: https://github.com/pageton/gotg
- Documentation: https://pkg.go.dev/github.com/pageton/gotg
- Examples: https://github.com/pageton/gotg/tree/main/examples
- Telegram Chat: https://t.me/gotg_community
- Changelog: ./CHANGELOG.md
- Contributing: ./CONTRIBUTING.md

## AI Agent Notes
- All exported types/functions have godoc comments (run `go doc github.com/pageton/gotg` to preview)
- Example tests (`ExampleXxx`) are in `*_test.go` files
- Session encryption uses AES-256-GCM when `ClientOpts.SessionEncryptionKey` is set
- Default parse mode can be set via `ClientOpts.ParseMode` ("HTML", "MarkdownV2")
- Reconnection requires `ClientOpts.AutoReconnect` + `client.RunForever()` instead of `Idle()`
